'import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
from matplotlib.ticker import FuncFormatter

# Professional color palette
COLORS = {
    "bar_fill": "#7FA9C7",
    "py_line": "#6C757D",
    "gray_700": "#495057",
    "gray_600": "#6C757D",
    "positive": "#1971c2",
    "negative": "#c2255c"
}

# Get top 8 accounts
dataset_top = dataset.nlargest(8, "Order Lines")

# Calculate metrics
dataset_top["pct_change"] = (dataset_top["Order Lines"] - dataset_top["Order Lines (PY)"]) / dataset_top["Order Lines (PY)"]
dataset_top["arrow"] = dataset_top["pct_change"].apply(lambda x: "\u25B2" if x > 0 else "\u25BC")
dataset_top["label_color"] = dataset_top["pct_change"].apply(lambda x: COLORS["positive"] if x > 0 else COLORS["negative"])

# Format function
def format_thousands(val):
    if val >= 100000:
        return f"{round(val/1000)}K"
    elif val >= 10000:
        return f"{val/1000:.1f}K"
    else:
        return f"{val/1000:.2f}K"

# Create data labels
dataset_top["data_label"] = dataset_top.apply(
    lambda row: f"{format_thousands(row['Order Lines'])} ({row['arrow']}{abs(row['pct_change'])*100:.0f}%)",
    axis=1
)

# Reverse order for bottom-to-top display
dataset_top = dataset_top.iloc[::-1].reset_index(drop=True)

# Create figure
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6.3))

max_val = dataset_top["Order Lines"].max()
x_range = [-max_val * 0.35, max_val * 1.18]

y_positions = np.arange(len(dataset_top))

# Draw bars
ax.barh(
    y_positions,
    dataset_top["Order Lines"],
    height=0.65,
    color=COLORS["bar_fill"],
    alpha=0.65
)

# Draw PY comparison lines
for i, row in dataset_top.iterrows():
    ax.plot(
        [row["Order Lines (PY)"], row["Order Lines (PY)"]],
        [i - 0.4, i + 0.4],
        color=COLORS["py_line"],
        linewidth=3,
        solid_capstyle="round"
    )

# Add data labels outside bars
for i, row in dataset_top.iterrows():
    ax.text(
        row["Order Lines"] + (max_val * 0.02),
        i,
        row["data_label"],
        va="center", ha="left",
        fontsize=8,
        fontweight="bold",
        color=row["label_color"]
    )

# Add account names on left
for i, row in dataset_top.iterrows():
    ax.text(
        -max_val * 0.02,
        i,
        row["Key Account Name"],
        va="center", ha="right",
        fontsize=9,
        fontweight="bold",
        color=row["label_color"]
    )

# Configure axes
ax.set_yticks([])
ax.set_xlim(x_range)
ax.set_xlabel("Order Lines", fontsize=11, color=COLORS["gray_700"])

# X-axis formatting
def format_k(x, pos):
    if x >= 0:
        return f"{int(round(x/1000))}K"
    return ""

ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(FuncFormatter(format_k))

# Clean styling
ax.spines["top"].set_visible(False)
ax.spines["right"].set_visible(False)
ax.spines["left"].set_visible(False)
ax.spines["bottom"].set_visible(False)
ax.grid(False)

# Tick styling
ax.tick_params(
    axis="x",
    labelsize=10,
    colors=COLORS["gray_600"],
    length=0
)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()'
